steps.title: Multiply the functions
steps.content:
Use the given expressions for $f(x)$ and $g(x)$:
$$
f(x)g(x)=(x^2+bx)(8x^2-40x)
$$
$$
f(x)g(x)=8x^4-40x^3+8bx^3-40bx^2
$$
$$
f(x)g(x)=8x^4+(-40+8b)x^3-40bx^2
$$

steps.title: Match the coefficients
steps.content: Compare with $8x^4-39x^3-5x^2$. Using the $x^3$ coefficient: $$ -40+8b=-39 $$ $$ 8b=1 $$ $$ b=\frac{1}{8} $$ The answer is C.
